title: annona andicola
intro: Annona andicola, also known as the Peruvian custard apple, is a species of flowering plant in the Annonaceae family. This plant has been used for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties, which have been attributed to the presence of annonaceous acetogenins. The health benefits of Annona andicola include its potential to help manage diabetes, cancer, and neurodegenerative diseases, such as Alzheimer's and Parkinson's. Annona andicola can be used in various herbal preparations, including teas, infusions, and extracts, which have been traditionally used in folk medicine in South America.
